title: Ending A — The God of Fear & Hunger
text: Requires the Girl in the party. Destroy all three hearts of the God of the Depths, then descend to the Heart of Darkness. The Girl begins transforming into the God of Fear and Hunger over the course of a boss fight. The protagonist is defeated but acknowledged for their suffering. A voice tells them to lay down and rest so their life essence can usher in something new. The protagonist never left the dungeons, but played a vital part in birthing a new age of the God of Fear and Hunger. This is canonical — the Girl appears as this god in Termina. Any character, any difficulty except the S-endings.

title: Ending B — The Older One
text: Same path as Ending A but without the Girl. At the Heart of Darkness, the protagonist faces the Traces of Gro-goroth. Though the boss fight must be won, it is implied the protagonist cannot truly defeat the Old God, who merely stops to ask why they resist. Gro-goroth reveals 'glimpses of the shape everyone shares' — knowledge too vast for any human mind. The protagonist dies from the terror as their bodily functions shut down. Their biggest regret was ever entering the dungeons.

title: Ending C — The Yellow King
text: Requires Le'garde alive in the party. Collect the four New God souls (Tormented, Endless, Enlightened, Dominating). Defeat the Nameless for access to the Golden Temple. Kill Francois the Dominant One. At the Throne of Ascension, Le'garde reveals he was faking amnesia — godhood was always his goal. He sits on the throne and teleports away. In the Void, the protagonist finds Le'garde after ~100 years (from his perspective) as the Yellow King. Answer 'You are not my god!' and kill him. Party escapes the dungeons forever scarred.

title: Ending C-II — The Prophecy
text: Same path as Ending C, but when Le'garde offers godhood in the Void, kneel before him. He says 'Yes... Love your god.' No final fight needed. Party escapes the dungeons but gained nothing. Years later, rumors spread of the Knights of the Midnight Sun returning. Le'garde unifies the western world, making every right decision as if guided by a higher power. He is throned as the Yellow King, bringing unprecedented prosperity. Decades later, the protagonist glimpses Le'garde — same age but with sickly yellow skin — and concludes he has become something else entirely.

title: Ending D — A New God
text: Same path as Ending C but without Le'garde. At the Throne of Ascension, without Le'garde present, the protagonist sits and is transported to the Void where they encounter their own God-like version. The God-like asks how they will rule as a god. Regardless of answers, the playthrough ends with the protagonist ascending to godhood. Answer variants determine the text epilogue describing the protagonist's divine reign.

title: Ending E — Escape?
text: Requires finding Le'garde (dead or alive). After discovering his fate, it becomes possible to leave the dungeons from the entrance path at any time. The protagonist and party escape, but remain haunted — their senses indicate they are still within the dungeons. A vision of a Guard manifests. It is left ambiguous whether the protagonist is hallucinating still being in the dungeons, or if the escape itself was the hallucination.

title: S Endings — Character-Specific Fates
text: Four S-endings exist, one per character, achievable only on Hard difficulty and not as a Marriage. Cahara's 'Happy Ending': find all three treasures from Isayah's maps in Ma'habre and the King's Crown, then leave the dungeons. Ragnvaldr's ending involves killing Le'garde and escaping with the Cube of the Depths. D'arce's ending involves saving Le'garde. Enki's ending involves ascending through knowledge. Each can only be achieved with the corresponding starting character on Hard mode.
